headline: Republican lawmakers are trading like Trump, piling into Big Tech stocks
summary: Republican lawmakers are adjusting their investment portfolios to keep up with President Trump's latest trades, the US leader favoring Nvidia (NVDA), Intel (INTC), and other Big Tech names. Yahoo Finance Washington Correspondent Ben Werschkul reports on this trend, giving rise to renewed concerns around Congressional stock trading.

content.title: Republican lawmakers are trading like Trump, piling into Big Tech stocks
content.description:
Republican lawmakers are adjusting their investment portfolios to keep up with President Trump's latest trades, the US leader favoring Nvidia (NVDA), Intel (INTC), and other Big Tech names.

Yahoo Finance Washington Correspondent Ben Werschkul reports on this trend, giving rise to renewed concerns around Congressional stock trading.

headline: Taiwan's MediaTek says it supports both TSMC and Intel's advanced packaging technologies
summary: Taiwan chip designer MediaTek said on Friday it supports both TSMC's and Intel's ‌advanced packaging technologies, allowing customers to choose between the ‌two approaches.  CoWoS, or Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate, is TSMC's advanced packaging technology widely used for artificial intelligence chips, including those designed ‌by Nvidia.

CoWoS, or Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate, is TSMC's advanced packaging technology widely used for artificial intelligence chips, including those designed ‌by Nvidia.

headline: Intel, 3DGS to set up $3.3 billion substrate plant in India's Odisha state
summary: U.S. chipmaker Intel and 3DGS Inc. ‌USA will invest about $3.3 billion ‌to set up a substrate manufacturing plant ​in the eastern Indian state of Odisha, the Indian government said on Friday.  • Substrates ⁠are the bedrock material on which elements of a semiconductor device ​are attached.  • ​New Delhi ​has pledged billions ‌of dollars in subsidies to attract semiconductor plants and related manufacturing as a part of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's wider push to build ‌more products locally.

content.title: Intel, 3DGS to set up $3.3 billion substrate plant in India's Odisha state
content.summary: U.S. chipmaker Intel and 3DGS Inc. ‌USA will invest about $3.3 billion ‌to set up a substrate manufacturing plant ​in the eastern Indian state of Odisha, the Indian government said on Friday.  • Substrates ⁠are the bedrock material on which elements of a semiconductor device ​are attached.  • ​New Delhi ​has pledged billions ‌of dollars in subsidies to attract semiconductor plants and related manufacturing as a part of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's wider push to build ‌more products locally.
